commands.review_summary:
The PR wires `tailscale_authkey` into Linux Tailscale setup, sets the Tailscale operator to `openclaw`, updates manual guidance, and adds `tailscale set --operator`/`tailscale serve` sudoers entries.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection. Current main declares and documents `tailscale_authkey` without using it, and the PR diff shows the new operator grant runs in the Tailscale task before user creation.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human and contributor follow-up because missing real behavior proof is a merge gate and the Tailscale permission boundary requires maintainer acceptance.

target.title: fix: wire tailscale_authkey and set operator for serve mode
target.body:
Closes #34

## Summary

Fix the Tailscale permission error that prevents the `openclaw` service user from running `tailscale serve`. Also wire the documented but unused `tailscale_authkey` variable into the Tailscale setup tasks.

## Problem

When `tailscale_enabled: true` is set and OpenClaw attempts to use Tailscale serve mode, the `openclaw` user gets:

```
serve failed: Command failed: /usr/bin/tailscale serve --bg --yes 18789
sending serve config: Access denied: serve config denied
Use 'sudo tailscale serve --bg --yes 18789'.
To not require root, use 'sudo tailscale set --operator=$USER' once.
```

Two issues exist on current main:

1. The scoped sudoers for the `openclaw` user include `status`, `up`, `down`, `ip`, `version`, `ping`, and `whois`, but not `serve` or `set --operator`. OpenClaw needs `tailscale serve` to expose services on the Tailnet.

2. The `tailscale_authkey` variable is declared in `defaults/main.yml` and documented in the README, but no task ever uses it. Users who set the auth key expecting automatic Tailscale connection still see manual setup instructions.

Both gaps were introduced when the Tailscale support was first added (sheeek, 2026-01-10, `6d3c52e2`) and when scoped sudoers were introduced (Andy Lauppe, 2026-02-10, `1f605546`).

## Changes

**`roles/openclaw/tasks/tailscale-linux.yml`:**
- Add auto-connect task that runs `tailscale up --authkey` when the auth key is provided and Tailscale is not yet connected. Uses `no_log: true` to prevent credential exposure.
- Add operator grant task that runs `tailscale set --operator={{ openclaw_user }}` after auto-connect, allowing the service user to run `tailscale serve` without sudo.
- Update the manual setup message to only show when no auth key is provided, and include the `tailscale set --operator` hint.

**`roles/openclaw/tasks/user.yml`:**
- Add `tailscale set --operator=*` and `tailscale serve *` to the scoped sudoers permissions, matching the existing wildcard pattern used for `up *`, `ip *`, `ping *`, etc.

## Validation

- `yamllint` passes on both changed files
- `ansible-lint` passes (production profile) on both changed files
- `ansible-playbook playbook.yml --syntax-check` passes
- No change for users who do not set `tailscale_authkey` (all new tasks are gated)

## Relation to PR #43

PR #43 adds Netbird VPN support and partially overlaps by wiring `tailscale_authkey`. However, PR #43 does not set the Tailscale operator, which is the root cause of the reported error in issue #34. This PR is a minimal, targeted fix that can merge independently.
target.author: sebtardif
